hook_text: Oman's payroll compliance isn't getting easier. It's getting stricter. And most companies aren't ready.
body:
The Ministry of Manpower has digitized inspection workflows.

What used to be a paper audit trail is now a data comparison:
Your submitted records vs. Ministry-held records vs. WPS bank data.

Discrepancies that used to slip through now create automatic flags.

Three areas where standards are tightening:

1. Profession code accuracy
The gap between registered code and actual job function is now a priority audit item. Companies with mismatches are being flagged proactively.

2. Omanization ratio reporting
Manual self-reporting is being cross-referenced against WPS data. Inconsistencies trigger review.

3. End-of-service fund compliance
For companies subject to new requirements, documentation must match payment records exactly.

The companies struggling aren't negligent. They're running on tools built for the old compliance model.

Spreadsheets worked when inspectors came once a year and checked paper files.

They don't work when Ministry systems are cross-referencing your data automatically.

---

Is your HR stack built for today's compliance environment — or 2018's?
cta: DM "COMPLIANCE" for a free gap analysis.
